ksa_text: Knowledge of LLM-specific security vulnerabilities and risks, including prompt injection, jailbreaking, insecure output handling, training data poisoning, model theft, prompt leakage, and model supply chain security.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.

top_anchors.element_label: AI risks and benefits from third-party entities are managed.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA SP-K-012 (SP, Knowledge) does not clear the weak fit inclusion threshold of 0.52. Composite score 0.4508 is insufficient to establish relevance to analytics and business intelligence at IC-to-senior-IC level. Framework signals (eu_ai_act=0.451, dcwf=0.423, nice=0.420) do not demonstrate sufficient alignment with core analytics/BI work.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: P2 exclude upheld. Score 0.4508 below threshold for weak fit domain; no rescuing analytics text signals. No internal inconsistencies identified.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External validation: does SP-domain KSA SP-K-012 (composite 0.4508) appear in published data analyst or BI role profiles?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: SP domain content (AI governance, security/privacy, risk management, compliance) is not a primary expectation in IC-to-senior-IC ANL roles per NIST CSF, ISO 27001; security competencies in IC ANL scope limited to data access controls awareness, not security architecture. Composite 0.4508 confirms weak signal. Exclusion consistent with published ANL frameworks.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: P3 exclude upheld. External evidence confirms SP domain KSA at composite 0.4508 is not a standard requirement for Data Analyst at IC-to-senior-IC level.

decision_rationale: Pipeline consensus: exclude. P2 exclude confirmed by P3 (internal) and P4 (external) adversarial agents. Zero overturns. Composite 0.4508, domain SP, scope weak fit. High confidence.
